sourceMovie.title: Lethal Weapon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Lethal Weapon (1987) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Richard Donner's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lethal Weapon 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lethal Weapon clicked for you, Lethal Weapon 2 (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lethal Weapon?
faq.answer: Try Lethal Weapon 2, Lethal Weapon 3, Lethal Weapon 4—each shares DNA with Lethal Weapon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lethal Weapon?
faq.answer: Lethal Weapon (1987) is best described as Action, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lethal Weapon part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Lethal Weapon Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
